nameMeanings.name: Jordon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jordan
meanings: English, German, French (mainly Alsace and Haute-Savoie), Polish, Czech, and Slovenian; Spanish and Hungarian (Jordán): from the Christian personal name or nickname Jordan. This is taken from the name of the river Jordan (Hebrew Yarden, a derivative of yarad ‘to go down’, i.e. to the Dead Sea). At the time of the Crusades it was a common practice for crusaders and pilgrims to bring back flasks of water from the river in which John the Baptist had baptized people, including Christ himself, and to use it in the christening of their own children. As a result Jordan became quite a common personal name.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard Jordon was born in 1149, in Malton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son with Jordan Foliot II. She died in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard Jordon est née en 1149, à Malton, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Jordan Foliot II. Elle est décédée dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard Jordon wurde 1149 in Malton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hatte mit Jordan Foliot II mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard Jordon è nata il nel 1149 nel Malton, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Jordan Foliot II. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.Malton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingd: イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアマルトン
ja.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard Jordonは1149年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアマルトンで生まれた。彼女はJordan Foliot IIとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard Jordon(은)는 1149년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 맬턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Jordan Foliot II(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard Jordon nasceu em 1149, em Malton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Jordan Foliot II. Ela faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard Jordon родилась в 1149 г. в местности малтон, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Jordan Foliot II. Она умерла в её родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs. Richard Jordon nació en 1149, en Malton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Jordan Foliot II. Murió en su pueblo.
